Re: Your Request for Advice
Our Fife No. A-95-052
Dear Mr. ordos:
You have requested advice on behalf of yourself and Ben
Davidian, about the "revolving doortr restrictions of the Political
Reform Act (the nActtr)1 that apply to your activities in private
practice after leaving the Fair Po1itical Practj-ces Commission
(the t'commission" ) .
OUESTTON
':a what restrictions does the Act place on your ability to
represent clients before the Cornmission, particularl-y during the
f j-rst year after leaving the comrnission?
coNcrrgsr 0N
You may not participate at any time in the future in any
enforcement proceedi.ng or litigation that began while you worked
at the commilsion. During the one year following your separation,
you rnay not, for compensation, represent a client for the purpose
of influencing legislative or administrative action of the
Cornnission.
FACTS
You held the position of Executive Director of the Fair
Political Practices commission from November 12, 1991 to ,fanuary
6, f995- Mr. Davidian served as Chairman of the Commission frorn

cor..rrnent code sections 8l,ooo-91015. A1I statutory

commission regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations,
Sections 18000-1s954. A11 references to regulations are to
Title 2, Division 6 of the california code of Regulations.

uarch 12, Lggl-t until leaving office at the expiration of his
four-year term on January 13, 1995. You are both currently
emploted as attorneys hlith the Sacramento Law firn of wilke,
fleury, Hoffe1t, could and Birney. You antici.pate that a portion
of your practice t i1l involve the repJacsentation of clients who
are- reg-r.r1ated by the Act or who have some interest in the official
actions of the Commission.
ANAIJYSIS
I. 
PerEanent Ban on ParticiDating ir SDecific ProceediDas.
Sections 87400-87405 of the Act permanently prohibit you
from partj-cipating in certain proceedings bet$reen specific parties
in whlch you were j-nvolved as Executive Director and chairman of
the conmission. SpecificallY, You may not act as attorney or
iq""t i"i or otherivise repr"ienl or advise any person2 for
c6rnpensation, before the commission, a state agency, or.court, in
a "judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceeding"s in which you
parlicipated at the Cornrnissj.on and in which the state of
Calj-fornia is a party. 
(sections 87401 and A74O2. ) 
The
Comnission's enforcenent proceedings, fitigation, 
formal opinion
requests, and contracts are the types of matters which come within
th; definition of "judicj,aI, quasi-judicial or other proceedj"ng. "
(section 87400 (c) , )
j{l
The adoption, amendment, or repeal- of regul-ations are not
considered "judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedings'r covered
by these statutes, because they involve the formul'ation of rules
oi general application to be apptied prospectively and not the
rights or clains of specific parties. 
(Huston Advice Letter, No.
A-84-002. ) 
(Your participation in comrnission regulations,
hoLrever, i-s restricted by the one-year ban di-scussed below.)
Further, you may not aid, advise, counsel, consult or assist
in representing any other person with the intent to influence the
outcome of a proceeding in which you participated. 
(Section
a74o2. ) This means that you may not work on proceedings you have

any other organization or group of persons acting in concert. rr

section 87400(c) defines such agency proceedings to include:
trany proceeding, applicati-on, request for a ruling or other
determination, contract, cIaim, controversy, investigation,
charge, accusation, arrest or other particular matter involving a
specific party or parties in any court or state administrative
agency, including but not linited to any proceeding,' governed by

partj.cipated in at the conmission as outside attorneys and you may
not assist or advise ottrer attorneys in your firn or clients
themsefves in working on such matters.
You are considered to have "partricipatedl in a proceeding
during your tenure with the Corunission if you took part in the
proceeding rrpersonally and substantially through decision,
lpproval, -diiapproval, formal written.recommendation, renderingT
aavice on a substantial basis, investigation or use of
confidential infornation. i' (Section 87400(d).) This covers any
proceeding in which you actually participated while at the
comrnission as well as any proceeding which you supervised or which
was handled by the Commission staff during your tenure. (Brown
Advice Letter, No. A-91-033.) Because of your manageroent
positions over all staff as Executive Director and chairman, any
iroceeding that began during your tenure at the comrnission !ril1 be
subject to the permanent ban.
The permanent ban restricts only compensated activity, it
does not prevent you from representing or assisting any person on
a volunteer basis. Further, it does not prevent you from
representing the state or a state agency- (Sections 87401 and
874O2.)
Section 87403 contains sorne additional narrow exemptions frorn
thE permanent revolving door prohibitions. 
These generall'y al1ow
you to provide uncompensated expert witness testinony or, in
specifil circumstances, and \.rrith the perrnission of the Commission
oi a court, to participate more acti.vely in a proceeding which was
pendinq during your tenure with the Commission-
II. 
one-Year Ban on Influencinq Adluinistrative or
Leaislative Action or contracts.
In addition to permanentl-y being barred frorn working on
proceedings that you participated in at the Conmission, you are
llso subject to a one-year ban on influencing certain commission
actions. covernnent code Section 87406(d) states:
(1) No ... member of a state administrative
agency, for a period of one year after leaving office or
employment, shaII, for compensation, act as agent or
attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person,
by making any formal or informal appearance, or by
naking any oral or written communication, before any
state administrative agency, or officer or empLoyee
thereof, for which he or she worked or represented
during the 12 months before l-eaving off j-ce or
employnent, if the appearance or communication is made
for the purpose of influencing adninistrative or
Iegislative action .... 
For purposes of this paragraph,
an appearance before a state administrative agency does
not include an appearance in a court of law [or] before
an administrative 1aw judge ....

Thus, for one year after Your
Commission, you may not be paid to
cornmunicate with the Commission or
of:
departure from the
appear before or
its staff for the purpose
o influencing{ adrninistrative action,'
o influencing legislative actions; or
o influencing any action or proceeding involving the
awarding or revocation of a contract, or the sale or
purchase of gtoods or property.
(section 87406 (d) . )
A. Adninistrative Action.
"Administrative action" is defined as rrthe proposal,
drafting, development, consideration, amendrnent, enactment or
defeat by any state agency of any ruIe, regulation or other action
in any rate-makingT proceeding or any quas i-1egis lative proceeding,
which shal-] include any [rulemaking] proceeding" governed by
Government code section 11340 et seq. (Section 82oo2. ) Thus, you
are prohibited for one year after leaving office from appearlng
bef,ore or communicating with the Cornrn.ission or its staff to
inffuence the commj-ssion's regulatory adoption process.
1. Enforcement Proceedinqs.
Requlation 18202 provides that an enforcement proceeding is
not a quas i-legislative 
proceeding. This does not answer
precisely the question whether such proceedings fall within the
somewhat broader definition of "administrative actionrr in section
87406(d). However, given that: (1) the revolving door l-aw was
designed to prevent lobbving of former agencies by legislative and
executive branch officials, 
(2) the lanquage in the definition of
4 
Section 82032 defines rrinfluencing legislative or
adninistrative action'r as "promotj-ng, supporting, influencing,
rnodifying, opposing or delaying any legislative or administrative
action by any means, including but not linited to the provision or
use of inforrnation, statistics, studies or analyses.rr
5 
Section €12037 defines "legislatj-ve actionrr as rtthe drafting,
introduction, consideration, nodification, enactnent or defeat of
any biI1, resolution, amendment, report, nomination or other
matter by the Legislature or by either house or any committee,
subconmittee, joint or select commj-ttee thereof, or by a nenber or
employee of the Legislature acting in his official 
capacity.
'Legislative action' also neans the action of the Governor in
approving or vetoing any biII."

Page 5
rradministrative actionrr does not appear to encompass enforcenent
or other quasi-judicial actions, and (3) enforcement actions eIg
covered by the permanent ban (and there is no overlap in coverage
between the permanent and one-year bans), it is our opinion that
no aspect of Commission enforcenent aations are covered by the
one-year ban. In light of your recent positions as Executive
Director and chairman of the commission, however, hre trust that
both you and commission staff will roake every effort to avoid even
the appearance of any impropriety or special access.
2. Reguestino rvritten Ailvice.
A difficult 
aspect of the "revolving doorrt law for forner
Commissioners and designated employees of the Fair Political
Practices Commission is their involvement in seeking verbal or
written advice frorn the agency during the one-year ban. There is
a specific exemption in commission regulations for tr[a] proceeding
involving the issuance of a legal opinion." 
(Regulation
18202(a) (7).) 
such proceedings are deemed not to be 'rquasi-
legislative action.tr we interpret this exceptj-on to appLy to
formal opinions of the Cornmissiono (see section 83114(a)), but not
to written advice (see Section 83114(b)). The fornulation of
written advice is not a rrproceedingrr and is not conducted in a
fornal, recorded manner. In addition, it has far-reachingt
irnplicatlons, including the potential for providinq immunity from
pxosecution for you or your clients (see Section 83114).
Although the specific exception in Regulation 78202 does not
apply to wrj-tten advj-ce, it does not appear to fa1I within the
definition of rradministrative action.rr However, because
Commission advice Letters can have quas i-legis lative aspects, and
to avoid any appearance of i.mpropriety, you may request wrj-tten
advice under the fol-l-owing conditions during the one-year ban.
Your requests for written advice should be in writing and not
suppJ-emented verbally by you to staff or any Commissioner
regarding your view of the facts, the applicable law, or the
correct outcone. If cLarification of the advice is necessary,
that request also must be in writing.
You are free to seek telephonic advice frorn Cornmission staff
if you do not plan to seek written advice, sj-nce telephonic advice
normally involves technical natters and does not grant irnmunity to
the requestor.

057.) Thii prohibition does not prevent you from comnunicating

In addition, the restrictions on .influencing adroinistrative
or teqislative action do not apply to paid or unpaid assistance
you render to a third person who subsequently appears before or
communicates with the Cornmission. Thus, the ban does not restrict
your assisting or advising other attorneys in your firn or clients
Lhemselves who night appear before or conmunicate with the
commission regarding a regulation or legislation under
consideration.
The restrictions of the one-year ban apply to cornpensated
activity, but not to volunteer work. They do not apply to paid or
unpaid appearances in court or before an administrative Ia!, judge-
section -e7aoo also provides an exception which would permit you to
contact the Commission regarding regulations and legislation if
you become an officer or employee of another state agency and your
co.ntact with the commission was on behalf of that agency.

Page 6
III.
Possible Reqistration as Lobbvists.
Your letter stated that you and Mr. Davidian have not yet
registered as lobbyists but rnight consj-der doing so in trle future.
You asked erhether the applicable revolving door restrictions woul-d
affect potential lobbying activities. 
In general, the Act's
revolving door restrictions $rould not prohibit you from lobbying
the LegisJ,ature or Governor.
The permanent ban of Sections 87401 and 87402 applies only to
judicial, quasi-judicial or other proceedings before any court or
state administrative agency in which you participated while at the
commission. Section 87400(a), hor,rever, expressly defines trstate
administrative agencyrr to exclude the Legislature. In addition,
developing legislative proposals would not be tt judicial or quasi-
judicialtt proceedings. (witherspoon Advice Letter, No. A-94-371, )
With respect to influencing adrninistrative or legislative
action, the one-year ban of Section 87406 only prohibits you fron
appearing before or communicating with the state agency for which
you worked. Thus, lobbying the Legislature or covernor regarding
legislation, including the PoIitlcaI Reform Act or other
legislation on which the Connission has taken a position, is not
restricted by Section 87406. (See Witherspoon Advice Letter, No.
A-94-371.)


File No. A-95-052
Page 7
Iv. 
aur[malv of Prohibited Activities.
As a result of the permanent and one-year revolvi-ng door
restrictions, you are prohibited from appearing before the
Commission or comrnunicating with Commission rnembers or staff in
the following types of matters:
o Any comrnission enforcement matter comrnenced duringT your
tenure with the commission.
o Any formaf opinion request submitted to the Cornmission
during your tenure there.
o Any litigation to which the Commission was a party during
your tenure with the Commission.
o Adoption, amendment, or repeal of any Commission
regulat.ions for one year after you left office-
o Any legislation being proposed or considered by the
Comrnission or its staff for one year after you l-eft office.
o Any contract considered by the Commission or its staff for
one year aiter you left office. 
After the one-year period has
expiied, you stil-I may not partj,cipate in any Commission action on
a -Lontracl which was considered by the commission or its staff
during your tenure.
v- 
gummarv of Permittedl Activities.

You may
participate in conmission hearings and meetings and contact

of f i,ce.
